stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 188.5 % and a standard deviation of 13.6 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 1.6% and 6.6%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 188.5 %. After correcting for the 4.1% bias: ≈ 196.6 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.6%-6.6%) and sampling variability: [186.6, 206.8]. Point estimate: 196.6 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
